_comment: https://github.com/rolldown/rolldown/issues/9806 — with experimental.lazyBarrel (default ON), a sideEffects:false barrel has an own derived statement `const Bar = Foo.Bar;` reading a property off a default-imported CJS submodule. The entry imports ONLY an unrelated re-export (`Other`), so lazyBarrel defers `./foo.cjs` (its exports aren't requested) and the `Foo` import record gets resolved_module=None. But `Foo.Bar` survives tree-shaking as a side-effectful property read, and the finalizer drops the unresolved import while the reading statement remains => `ReferenceError: Foo is not defined` at eval. Executes to assert the bundle runs (Other === 'other-value'); fails with the bug, passes once the loader stops deferring records referenced by retained barrel-own statements.
